Sub-product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
Consumer complaint narrative:
I opened a credit card and a credit history tracking subscription through a company called XXXX XXXX. Upon paying the subscription fee of {$70.00} per month, and also connecting my bank account details and sharing my social security number, the website seemed like a scam because nothing was functioning correctly. My credit score couldnt be pulled, when I connected my account, on some of their sites it would show up, but on the main page it would continue asking me to link my accounts. In addition, The website kept telling me my password was incorrect which it was notand when I changed my password twice through their password reset it was clear their website was the problem, and not a user error. As a consumer financial service this is highly unnerving because of the confidential information they have. This made me want to cancel and delete my account all together. And this is where the biggest problem lies. As hey have a {$70.00} per month subscription, it doesnt allow users to change or cancel their subscription online on their account nor does it allow users to delete their private information. Instead it asks for consumers to go through a help email which goes unanswered for MONTH on end, XXXX THE WHILE STILL CHARGING THE SUBSCRIPTION FEE. WHICH IS FRAUD. 

This coupled with the fact that this company now has all my banking and social security details, makes me very uneasy and worried for not only my but other consumers safety.

Consumer complaint narrative:
My former landlord, XXXX XXXX in XXXX, issued me a {$950.00} refund check ( check number XXXX ) from their Wells Fargo account for a residential security deposit. They claimed the check was mailed to their own office in XXXX, but it was never received. However, the check was marked as cashed, even though I never received, endorsed, or deposited it. 

I reported this immediately. XXXX acknowledged the issue and provided XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX by XXXX Forged Endorsement. I completed, notarized, and mailed the affidavit, and it was confirmed delivered on XX/XX/year>. 

Despite having everything they need to reissue the payment, XXXX is refusing to return my deposit. They claim they can not act until Wells Fargo completes its internal investigation, which they say XXXX take up to XXXX  days. I currently have no visibility into the investigation or access to the funds owed to me. 

XXXX is using XXXX XXXX timeline to avoid fulfilling their legal obligation to return my deposit. I have provided all requested documentation, but they are choosing to delay reimbursement until the bank concludes its own process. This has left me without my money and without a clear path to resolution. 

I live in XXXX. The full {$1000.00} deposit has not been returned. I am seeking assistance in expediting this matter and ensuring that XXXX does not continue to use a third-party bank delay to withhold tenant funds.
